'''Zalso''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Journal4" /> located in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds']] [[Zalso system/Legends|Zalso system]].<ref name="TEA" /> The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Kiras Torla]], who graduated from the offworld [[Galactic Outdoor Survival School/Legends|Galactic Outdoor Survival School]]<ref name="AIR">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref> around [[12 BBY/Legends|12 BBY]]<ref name="AIRdates">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', which establishes that [[Kiras Torla]] graduated from the [[Galactic Outdoor Survival School/Legends|Galactic Outdoor Survival School]] approximately fourteen [[Standard year/Legends|standard years]] prior to its setting, represents a collection of [[in-universe]] reports from "one year before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]," which corresponds to approximately [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> and was middle-aged<ref name="AIR" /> around [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]],<ref name="AIRdates" /> was [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to Zalso.<ref name="AIR" /> It was also the homeworld of [[Ral Shawgrim]], a Human who was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]]<ref name="Journal4" /> at some point no earlier than [[16 BBY/Legends|16 BBY]]<ref name="16BBY">{{JournalCite|1|Wanted by Cracken (Star Wars Adventure Journal)}} establishes that the "[[Wanted by Cracken (Star Wars Adventure Journal)|Wanted by Cracken]]" segment of the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'' represents an [[in-universe]] update to the [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] ''[[Wanted by Cracken]]'', the original version of which is presented in the sourcebook [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']]. The latter work is dated to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], and since ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates that conflict to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the placement of the sourcebook therefore translates to [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]. Furthermore, the installment of "Wanted by Cracken" in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 4|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 4]] establishes [[Ral Shawgrim]] is twenty-three years old, meaning he must have been [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] at some point no earlier than [[16 BBY/Legends|16 BBY]].</ref> aboard a [[Panorama|luxury liner]] in<ref name="Journal4" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']]<ref name="OuterRim">{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-outer-rim-sectors|text=The Essential Atlas Extra: Outer Rim Sectors|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> [[Tion Hegemony/Legends|Tion Hegemony]].<ref name="Journal4" />

By approximately [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="1 ABY">{{JournalCite|4|Wanted by Cracken (Star Wars Adventure Journal)}} establishes that [[Ral Shawgrim]] contacted the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] cell on Zalso at least around two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior to the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], which corresponds to some point by approximately [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] cell operated on Zalso.<ref name="Journal4" /> At some point by that date,<ref name="1 ABY" /> Shawgrim's [[Parent/Legends|parents]] were employed there during the [[cor'tan]] season. Eventually, [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] [[Death/Legends|killed]] them, which led to Shawgrim contacting the Zalso Rebel cell and becoming a member of the Alliance. Zalso's role in Shawgrim's biography was later noted by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] in an entry of the [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] ''[[Wanted by Cracken]]''<ref name="Journal4" />—the initial version of which was published in [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]<ref name="16BBY" />—dedicated to Shawgrim.<ref name="Journal4" />

==Behind the scenes==
Zalso was first mentioned in the "[[Wanted by Cracken (Star Wars Adventure Journal)|Wanted by Cracken]]" segment of the [[November]] [[1994]] [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 4|fourth issue]] of [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]''. The segment was authored by [[Craig Robert Carey|C. Robert Carey]].<ref name="Journal4" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Zalso [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet itself, in grid square M-11.<ref name="TEA" />
